BreakfastIngredients: Whole wheat flour, spinach (iron), jaggery, milk
BreakfastPreparation: Mix whole wheat flour with a pinch of salt and water to form a soft dough | Prepare spinach filling: chop finely, add salt, cumin, and green chilli | Divide dough into balls, flatten each, place filling in centre and seal edges | Roll out into round parathas of medium thickness | Heat a tawa on medium flame, cook paratha with ghee until golden spots appear on both sides | Serve hot with curd or pickle
LunchPreparation: Soak rajma (kidney beans) overnight in plenty of water | Drain and pressure cook with salt for 5-6 whistles until completely soft | Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds | Add finely chopped onion and sauté until deep golden | Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes | Add blended tomatoes, all spices, and cook until oil separates | Add boiled rajma with its water and bring to a boil | Simmer for 15-20 minutes until gravy thickens | Finish with butter or cream, garnish with coriander
DinnerPreparation: Knead bajra flour with warm water and a pinch of salt into a smooth dough | Cover and rest for 10 minutes | Divide into small balls, dust with flour, and roll out evenly | Cook on a hot tawa for 30 seconds each side, then roast directly on flame for a few seconds | Apply a little ghee and serve warm

BreakfastPreparation: Bring 1 cup milk (or water) to a gentle boil in a saucepan | Add rolled oats and stir well | Cook on medium-low heat for 3-5 minutes, stirring occasionally until thick | Add dates and jaggery and mix well | Top with soaked nuts or seeds as per ingredients | Serve warm immediately
LunchPreparation: Wash and soak rice and chana dal together for 20 minutes | Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds and let them splutter | Add finely chopped onion, ginger, and garlic — sauté until golden | Add chopped tomato, turmeric, and salt, cook for 2 minutes | Drain and add soaked rice-dal mix, stir well | Add 3 cups water, close lid and pressure cook for 3-4 whistles | Let pressure release naturally, open and mix gently | Add a dollop of ghee and serve hot with curd or pickle
DinnerPreparation: Knead whole wheat flour with warm water and a pinch of salt into a smooth dough | Cover and rest for 10 minutes | Divide into small balls, dust with flour, and roll out evenly | Cook on a hot tawa for 30 seconds each side, then roast directly on flame for a few seconds | Apply a little ghee and serve warm

BreakfastPreparation: Soak moong dal for 2 hours if whole dal, then grind to a smooth batter | Add salt, turmeric, green chilli, ginger, and finely chopped vegetables | Adjust consistency with water to a thin, spreadable batter | Heat a non-stick pan, grease lightly with oil | Pour a ladleful of batter and spread in circular motion | Cook on medium heat until edges lift, flip and cook the other side | Serve hot with green chutney or curd
LunchPreparation: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, immediately transfer to ice cold water | Drain and blend spinach to a smooth puree | Cut paneer into cubes, shallow fry lightly until golden (optional) | Heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds | Add chopped onion, ginger, garlic and sauté until golden | Add tomato puree, salt, and spices — cook until oil separates | Pour spinach puree and bring to a gentle simmer | Add paneer cubes and simmer for 5 minutes | Finish with cream, adjust seasoning and serve

DinnerPreparation: Mix wheat flour with methi leaves, turmeric, red chilli, and curd to form a soft dough | Rest the dough for 10 minutes covered | Divide into equal balls and roll out into thin rounds | Heat a tawa and cook thepla on medium heat with a little oil until light golden on both sides | Serve warm with curd or green chutney

LunchPreparation: Wash dal thoroughly and soak for 15-20 minutes | Pressure cook with turmeric, salt, and water for 3-4 whistles until soft | Heat ghee in a small pan for tadka — add cumin, dried red chilli, and asafoetida | Add finely chopped onion, ginger, garlic and sauté until golden | Add chopped tomato and cook until soft and oil separates | Pour tadka over cooked dal, add garam masala, mix well | Adjust consistency with water if needed | Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with rice or roti
DinnerPreparation: Knead whole wheat flour with spinach puree, salt, and a little oil into a stiff dough | Divide into small balls and roll out into small circles | Heat oil in a deep pan until moderately hot | Fry each puri pressing gently so it puffs up, flip and fry until golden | Remove on absorbent paper and serve hot

DinnerPreparation: Press tofu between towels for 15 minutes to remove excess water | Cut into cubes or slices and season with soy sauce, turmeric, and spices | Heat oil in a pan on high heat | Add tofu in a single layer and fry without moving for 3-4 minutes until golden | Flip and fry the other side | Add vegetables, garlic, and sauce of your choice | Stir fry on high heat for 3-4 minutes | Serve hot over rice or with roti

LunchPreparation: Soak rajma (kidney beans) overnight in plenty of water | Drain and pressure cook with salt for 5-6 whistles until completely soft | Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds | Add finely chopped onion and sauté until deep golden | Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes | Add blended tomatoes, all spices, and cook until oil separates | Add boiled rajma with its water and bring to a boil | Simmer for 15-20 minutes until gravy thickens | Finish with butter or cream, garnish with coriander

LunchPreparation: Soak rajma (kidney beans) overnight in plenty of water | Drain and pressure cook with salt for 5-6 whistles until completely soft | Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds | Add finely chopped onion and sauté until deep golden | Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes | Add blended tomatoes, all spices, and cook until oil separates | Add boiled rajma with its water and bring to a boil | Simmer for 15-20 minutes until gravy thickens | Finish with butter or cream, garnish with coriander
